note: Internal sanity check only; NOT a verification step (that is delegated to /cdr:verify per invariant I4).

checks.details: All 5 new tests (TestRecordEncodeDecodeRoundTrip + 5 subtests, TestAsXxxTypeMismatch, TestFsyncBeforeApply, TestAppendAndApplyErrorFromApply, TestAppendAndApplyWriterErrorSkipsApply) pass, plus all 3 pre-existing 1.3.1 tests (TestSegmentWriter, TestAppendOversizedRecordHardErrors, TestOpenWriterResumesExistingSegments) still pass -- no regression.
No output -- both new files already gofmt-clean.

known_limitations_disclosed: engine/wal package does not import engine/catalog or engine/btree; CatalogPutPayload.Record is treated as an opaque pre-encoded blob and BTreeInsert/Delete payloads carry only (path, fileID) primitives. Decoding the opaque catalog record bytes back into a catalog.CatalogRecord, and actually invoking btree.Insert/Delete or catalog.Put/Delete as the 'apply' callback, is left to whichever future subtask wires this WAL layer into the catalog/btree call sites (not scoped to 1.3.2, which only had to define record types + the fsync-before-apply write path).

AppendAndApply's fsync-before-apply guarantee is proven empirically in TestFsyncBeforeApply via an independent ReadSegment call from inside the apply callback (real disk read, not a mock), rather than via a synthetic instrumented-fsync spy, since Writer (1.3.1) does not expose a fsync hook and was correctly left unmodified by this subtask.
